'''Dwarves''' (Khuzdul: ''Khazâd'') are a sturdy, long-lived race in [[Middle-earth]], created by the Vala Aulë before the awakening of the [[Elves]]. They are renowned as miners, smiths, and warriors, and they dwell in great underground halls and cities carved from stone.

The [[Dwarves]] are organised into seven kindreds, descended from the seven Fathers of the Dwarves. The most prominent kindred in [[The Lord of the Rings]] is '''Durin's Folk''' (the Longbeards), whose ancestral homes include Khazad-dûm ([[Moria]]) and Erebor (the Lonely Mountain).

Dwarves are secretive about their language (Khuzdul), fiercely loyal to their kin, and harbour an ancient rivalry with the [[Elves]] — a grudge that makes the friendship between [[Gimli]] and [[Legolas]] all the more remarkable.

== Notable Dwarves ==
* [[Gimli]] — son of Glóin, member of the [[Fellowship of the Ring]]
* Glóin — companion of [[Bilbo Baggins]] in ''[[The Hobbit]]''
* Thorin Oakenshield — leader of the quest to reclaim Erebor
* Durin the Deathless — the eldest of the Fathers of the Dwarves
* Balin — who led a colony to reclaim [[Moria]] and perished there

[[Category:Dwarves]]
